name: Georgetown Summer Honors

essays.prompt: Why are you interested in this specific program? What do you hope to gain from the experience?
tips: Georgetown values sophisticated, intellectually curious students — show you know what you're getting into

outcomes.college_impact: Positive signal at Georgetown specifically; well-regarded at Ivy League and other top schools for students interested in law, international affairs, or policy.
outcomes.typical_results: Strong representation at Georgetown, George Washington University, University of Virginia, and other schools with international affairs programs.

what_they_look_for: Interest in international affairs, law, or policy
Maturity for a college campus environment

blurbs.short: A 3-week pre-college summer program at Georgetown University across a range of disciplines including law, international affairs, and business.
blurbs.long: Georgetown Summer Honors is a set of 3-week intensive summer programs offered across many disciplines at Georgetown University. Unlike SSHI or TASP, Georgetown Honors is a broader program with many subject tracks. It is selective but not extremely so — approximately 200 students attend each summer across all tracks. The most popular tracks are in international affairs, law, and business. Georgetown's location in Washington DC gives students unique access to the policy world — speakers often include current and former government officials. The program is a good fit for students who want a serious academic preview of Georgetown specifically, and for students interested in international relations, security studies, or pre-law.

curriculum: Lectures from Georgetown faculty, discussions, site visits in DC (Congress, State Department, think tanks), collaborative projects.
